entry.title: 《Deep Learning》 Reading Nodes:1
entry.content: Linear Algebra(线性代数)Linear algebra is a branch(科) of mathematics(数学) that is widely used throughout science and engineering(工程). Scalars, Vectos, Matrices, Tensor, Identity and Inverse Matrices, Mutiplying Matrices and Vsctors, Linear Dependence and Span Scalars, Vectos, Matrices and Tensor Scalars(标量): A scalars means a single number. Vectos(向量): A vector id an array. The number are arranged in order.We wirte them as a column(列) enclosed in square brackets(方括号): Matrices(矩阵): A matrix is a 2-D array of number,so each is identified(可识别的) by two indices(标记) instead of just one.We write them as an array enclosed in square brackets: Tensors(张量): In the general case, an array of numbers arranged(安排) on a regular(规则的) grid(网格) with a variable(可变的) number of axes(轴) is known as a tensor. Transpose(转置) is one important operation(运算) on matrices. The transpose of a matrix is the mirror(镜子) image of the matrix across a diagonal(对角线) line, called the main diagonal(主对角线), running down and to the right, starting from its upper left corner. We denote the trandpose of matrix A as AT, and it is define such that Vectors(向量) can be thought of as matrices that contain(b包含) only one column. The transpose of a vector is therefore a matrix with only one row. Like x = [x1,x2,x3]T A scalar can be thought of as a matrix with only a single entry. A scalar is its own transpose: a = aT. The matrices can be added to each other, as long as they have same shape, just by adding their corresponding(对应的) elements: C = A + B where Ci,j = Ai,j + Bi,j. A scalar can add or mutiply a matrix by a scalar, just by performing(执行) that operation(运算) on each element of a matrix: D = a · B + c where Di,j = a · Bi,j + c. In deep learning, we allow the addition of matrix and a vector, yielding another matrix: C = A + b, where Ci,j = Ai,j + Bj.This implicit(隐式的) copying of b to many locations is called broadcastion(广播). Mutiplying Matrices and VsctorsThe matrix product(矩阵乘积) of matrices A and B is a third matrix C. A must have the same number of columns as B has rows. If A is of shape m n and **B is of shape n p. We can write the matrix product like *C = AB***. The product operation(运算) is defined by The product of two matrices is just a matrix containing(包含) the product of the individual(个别的) elements calles the element-wise product(元素对应乘积), or Hadamard product, and is denoted as A ⊙ B. The dot product between two vectors x and y of the same dimensionality(维度) is the matrix product xTy Identity and Inverse Matrices(单位矩阵和逆矩阵)The structure(结构) of the identity matrix(单位矩阵) is: all the entries(元素) along the main diagonal(对角线) are 1, while all the other entries are zero. The matrix inverse(逆矩阵) of A is denoted as A-1, and it is defined as the matrix such that: Now we can solve Ax = b follwing this: Linear Dependence and SpanFor Ax think of A as specifying(指定) different directions(方向) we can travel(移动) in from the origin(原点) (the point specified by the vector of all zeros), then determine(决定) how many ways there are of reaching b. In this view, each element of x specifying how far we should travel in each of these directions, with xi specifying how far to move in the direction of column i: This kind of operation is calles a linear combination(线性组合).Formally(形式上), a linear combination of some set of vectors {v(1), . . . ,v(n)} is given by multiplying each vector v(n) by a corresponding(相应的) scalar(标量) coefficient(系数) and adding the results: The span(生成子空间) of a set of vectors is the set of all points obtainable(可获得的) by linear combination of the original vectors. Determining(决定) wheter Ax = b has a solution thus amounts to testing whether b is in the span of the columns of A. This particular span is known as the column space(列空间), or the range(值域), of A. In order for the system Ax = b to have a solution for all values of b ∈ Rm.The requirement that the column space of A be all of Rm implies(暗示) immediately that A must have at least m columns, that is, n ≥ m. Otherwise, the dimensionality(维度) of the column space would be less than m. Having n ≥ m is only a necessary condition(条件) for every point to have a solution. It is not a suﬃcient(充分) condition, because it is possible for some of the columns to be redundant(冗余).Formally, this kind of redundancy is known as linear dependence(线性相关). A set of vectors is linearly independent(线性无关) if no vector in the set is a linear combination of the other vectors. A square(方阵) matrix with linearly dependent columns is known as singular(奇异的).
